---
title: "Research Paper"
subtitle: "MLSys·im: First-Principles Infrastructure Modeling for ML Systems"
---

## Abstract

MLSys·im is an open-source, first-principles analytical framework for predicting the performance, cost, and carbon footprint of machine learning systems — from edge microcontrollers to exascale GPU clusters. Unlike cycle-accurate simulators that require hours of compilation and weeks of runtime, MLSys·im resolves the 22 physical and logical constraints ("Systems Walls") that govern ML infrastructure in under 0.3 seconds. The framework introduces a 3-tier resolver architecture (Models, Solvers, Optimizers) with strict dimensional typing via Pint, enabling both human engineers and autonomous AI agents to reason quantitatively about hardware–software co-design. We validate against published MLPerf and vLLM benchmarks, demonstrating first-order accuracy across roofline analysis, LLM serving latency, distributed training scaling, and total cost of ownership. MLSys·im is the companion analytical engine for the *Machine Learning Systems* textbook (Harvard CS 249r) and is designed to serve as the ground-truth physics backend for agentic infrastructure design.

**Authors:** Vijay Janapa Reddi and contributors

---

## Key Contributions

1. **22 Systems Walls taxonomy** — A unified framework organizing the physical and logical constraints of ML systems into six domains (Node, Data, Algorithm, Fleet, Ops, Analysis)
2. **3-Tier resolver architecture** — Models ($Y = f(X)$), Solvers ($X = f^{-1}(Y)$), and Optimizers ($\max f(X)$ s.t. $g(X) \le c$) with strict dimensional typing
3. **Agent-native design** — MCP integration, JSON Schema export, and semantic exit codes enabling autonomous predict-compute-reflect loops
4. **Validated accuracy** — First-order agreement with MLPerf training benchmarks and vLLM serving measurements across diverse hardware

---

## Read the Paper

<object data="mlsysim-paper.pdf" type="application/pdf" width="100%" height="900px" style="border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 4px;">
  <p>Your browser does not support embedded PDFs.
  <a href="mlsysim-paper.pdf">Download the paper (PDF)</a>.</p>
</object>

::: {.callout-tip}
## Download
[**Download Paper (PDF)**](mlsysim-paper.pdf){.im-btn .im-btn-primary}
:::

---

## Citation

If you use MLSys·im in your research, please cite:

```bibtex
@article{reddi2025mlsysim,
  title     = {MLSys·im: First-Principles Infrastructure Modeling for Machine Learning Systems},
  author    = {Reddi, Vijay Janapa},
  year      = {2025},
  url       = {https://mlsysbook.ai/mlsysim/},
  note      = {Companion to the Machine Learning Systems textbook (Harvard CS 249r)}
}
```
